text: The surgeon prepares to start the intracorporeal anastomosis by identifying an ischemic zone on the specimen side.

The surgeon creates an enterotomy approximately three to four centimeters in size to accommodate the anvil, which was previously placed into the abdomen through the right lower quadrant port site. A gel port is used to seal the location, facilitating both extraction and robotic instrumentation.
The surgeon inserts the anvil into the collotomy, advancing the spike proximally along the bowel. A V-lock suture is used to secure the anvil's position and control its exit point.
The surgeon uses Firefly and Imogene to direct the spike's exit point, ensuring the V-lock is snug without loops and positioning the green anvil portion away from the staple line.

The surgeon pulls the anvil out using the V-lock suture. Once the anvil and V-lock are removed, the V-lock is used to close the colotomy on the specimen side and assist in removing the green anvil spike.
The surgeon uses the suture needle from the V-lock to remove the spike, inserting it in a specific manner and performing a quick pop-off to detach the spike.
